autoenable_created_entities.description: By default, all the entities are created enabled, but if you change the value of the autoenable_created_entities to false, the new entities will be created disabled.

domainId.description: DomainId to be used by the DomainParticipant.

ignore_non_matching_locators.description: Whether to ignore locators received on announcements from other participants when they don't match with any of the locators announced by this participant.

sendSocketBufferSize.description: Size in bytes of the send socket buffer. If the value is zero then Fast DDS will use the system default socket size.

listenSocketBufferSize.description: Size in bytes of the reception socket buffer. If the value is zero then Fast DDS will use the system default socket size.

participantID.description: DomainParticipant's identifier. Typically it will be automatically generated by the DomainParticipantFactory.

useBuiltinTransports.description: Boolean field to indicate the system whether the DomainParticipant will use the default builtin transports in addition to its userTransports.

avoid_builtin_multicast.description: Restricts multicast metatraffic to PDP only.

use_WriterLivelinessProtocol.description: Indicates whether to use the DataWriterLiveliness protocol.

readerPayloadSize.description: Maximum DataReader's History payload size. Allows to reserve all the required memory at DataReader initialization.

writerPayloadSize.description: Maximum DataWriter's History payload size. Allows to reserve all the required memory at DataWriter initialization.

mutation_tries.description: Number of different ports to try if DataReader's physical port is already in use.

leaseDuration_sec.description: Number of seconds. Indicates how long the DomainParticipant should consider remote DomainParticipants alive.

leaseDuration_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Indicates how long the DomainParticipant should consider remote DomainParticipants alive.

leaseAnnouncement_sec.description: Number of seconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its discovery message to all other discovered DomainParticipants as well as to all Multicast ports.

leaseAnnouncement_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its discovery message to all other discovered DomainParticipants as well as to all Multicast ports.

initialAnnouncements_count.description: Number of initial discovery messages to send at the period specified by <period>. After these announcements, the DomainParticipant will continue sending its discovery messages at the <leaseAnnouncement> rate.

initialAnnouncements_period_sec.description: Number of seconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its discovery messages.

initialAnnouncements_period_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its discovery messages.

clientAnnouncementPeriod_sec.description: Number of seconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its Discovery Message to its servers and check for EDP endpoints matching.

clientAnnouncementPeriod_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. The period for the DomainParticipant to send its Discovery Message to its servers and check for EDP endpoints matching.

simpleEDP_PUBWRITER_SUBREADER.description: Indicates if the participant must use Publication DataWriter and Subscription DataReader.

simpleEDP_PUBREADER_SUBWRITER.description: Indicates if the participant must use Publication DataReader and Subscription DataWriter.

typelookup_use_client.description: Create TypeLookup Service client builtin endpoints.

typelookup_use_server.description: Create TypeLookup Service server builtin endpoints.

remote_locators_max_unicast.description: Maximum number of unicast locators expected on a remote entity. It is recommended to use the maximum number of network interfaces available on the machine on which DomainParticipant is running.

remote_locators_max_multicast.description: Maximum number of multicast locators expected on a remote entity. May be set to zero to disable multicast traffic.

total_participants_initial.description: Number of elements for which space is initially allocated.

total_participants_maximum.description: Maximum number of elements for which space will be allocated.

total_participants_increment.description: Number of new elements that will be allocated when more space is necessary.

total_readers_initial.description: Number of elements for which space is initially allocated.

total_readers_maximum.description: Maximum number of elements for which space will be allocated.

total_readers_increment.description: Number of new elements that will be allocated when more space is necessary.

total_writers_initial.description: Number of elements for which space is initially allocated.

total_writers_maximum.description: Maximum number of elements for which space will be allocated.

total_writers_increment.description: Number of new elements that will be allocated when more space is necessary.

max_partitions.description: Maximum size of the partitions submessage. Set to zero for no limit.

max_user_data.description: Maximum size of the user data submessage. Set to zero for no limit.

max_properties.description: Maximum size of the properties submessage. Set to zero for no limit.

send_buffers_preallocated_number.description: Initial number of send buffers to allocate.

send_buffers_dynamic.description: Whether the number of send buffers is allowed to grow.

ignore_non_matching_locators.description: Whether to ignore locators received on announcements from other entities when they don't match with any of the locators announced by this DataWriter.

entityID.description: Sets the RTPSEndpointQos::entity_id of the RTPSEndpointQos class.

matchedSubscribersAllocation_initial.description: Number of elements for which space is initially allocated.

matchedSubscribersAllocation_maximum.description: Maximum number of elements for which space will be allocated.

matchedSubscribersAllocation_increment.description: Number of new elements that will be allocated when more space is necessary.

times_initialHeartbeatDelay_sec.description: Number of seconds. Initial heartbeat delay.

times_initialHeartbeatDelay_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Initial heartbeat delay.

times_heartbeatPeriod_sec.description: Number of seconds. Periodic heartbeat period.

times_heartbeatPeriod_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Periodic heartbeat period.

times_nackResponseDelay_sec.description: Number of seconds. Delay to apply to the response of an ACKNACK message.

times_nackResponseDelay_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Delay to apply to the response of an ACKNACK message.

times_nackSupressionDuration_sec.description: Number of seconds. This time allows the DataWriter to ignore NACK messages for a given period of time right after the data has been sent.

times_nackSupressionDuration_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. This time allows the DataWriter to ignore NACK messages for a given period of time right after the data has been sent.

ignore_non_matching_locators.description: Whether to ignore locators received on announcements from other entities when they don't match with any of the locators announced by this DataReader.

expectsInlineQos.description: It indicates if QoS is expected inline.

entityID.description: Set the RTPSEndpointQos::entity_id of the RTPSEndpointQos class.

matchedPublishersAllocation_initial.description: Number of elements for which space is initially allocated.

matchedPublishersAllocation_maximum.description: Maximum number of elements for which space will be allocated.

matchedPublishersAllocation_increment.description: Number of new elements that will be allocated when more space is necessary.

times_initialAcknackDelay_sec.description: Number of seconds. Initial ACKNACK delay.

times_initialAcknackDelay_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Initial ACKNACK delay.

times_heartbeatResponseDelay_sec.description: Number of seconds. Response time delay when receiving a Heartbeat.

times_heartbeatResponseDelay_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds. Response time delay when receiving a Heartbeat.

historyQos_kind.description: Fast DDS will only attempt to keep the latest values of the instance and discard the older ones. OR Fast DDS will attempt to maintain and deliver all the values of the instance to existing DataReaders.

historyQos_depth.description: It must be consistent with the ResourceLimitsQos <max_samples_per_instance> element value. It must be verified that: <depth> <= <max_samples_per_instance>.

resourceLimitsQos_max_instances.description: It defines the maximum number of instances.

resourceLimitsQos_allocated_samples.description: It controls the maximum number of samples to be stored.

resourceLimitsQos_extra_samples.description: The number of extra samples to allocate on the pool.

data_sharing_max_domains.description: Maximum number of Data-Sharing domain IDs in the local or remote endpoints.

deadline_period_sec.description: This QoS policy raises an alarm when the frequency of new samples falls below a certain threshold. On the publishing side, the deadline defines the maximum period in which the application is expected to supply a new sample. On the subscribing side, it defines the maximum period in which new samples should be received.

deadline_period_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for deadline period.

durability_kind.description: A DataWriter can send messages throughout a Topic even if there are no DataReaders on the network. Moreover, a DataReader that joins to the Topic after some data has been written could be interested in accessing that information. The DurabilityQoSPolicy defines how the system will behave regarding those samples that existed on the Topic before the DataReader joins.

latencyBudget_duration_sec.description: This QoS Policy specifies the maximum acceptable delay from the time the data is written until the data is inserted on the DataReader History and notified of the fact. That delay by default is set to 0 in order to optimize the internal operations.

latencyBudget_duration_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for latency budget duration.

lifespan_duration_sec.description: Each data sample written by a DataWriter has an associated expiration time beyond which the data is removed from the DataWriter and DataReader history as well as from the transient and persistent information caches. By default, the duration is infinite, which means that there is not a maximum duration for the validity of the samples written by the DataWriter.

lifespan_duration_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for lifespan duration.

liveliness_kind.description: This QoS Policy controls the mechanism used by the service to ensure that a particular entity on the network is still alive. There are different settings that allow distinguishing between applications where data is updated periodically and applications where data is changed sporadically.

liveliness_lease_duration_sec.description: Amount of time to wait since the last time the DataWriter asserts its liveliness to consider that it is no longer alive.

liveliness_lease_duration_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for liveliness lease duration.

liveliness_announcement_period_sec.description: Amount of time between consecutive liveliness messages sent by the DataWriter. This data member only takes effect if the kind is AUTOMATIC_LIVELINESS_QOS or MANUAL_BY_PARTICIPANT_LIVELINESS_QOS and needs to be lower than the lease_duration.

liveliness_announcement_period_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for liveliness announcement period.

ownership_kind.description: This QoS Policy specifies whether it is allowed for multiple DataWriters to update the same instance of data, and if so, how these modifications should be arbitrated.

ownershipStrength_value.description: This QoS Policy specifies the value of the strength used to arbitrate among multiple DataWriters that attempt to modify the same data instance. It is only applicable if the OwnershipQosPolicy kind is set to EXCLUSIVE_OWNERSHIP_QOS.

reliability_kind.description: This QoS Policy indicates the level of reliability offered and requested by the service.

reliability_max_blocking_time_sec.description: Configures the maximum duration that the write operation can be blocked.

reliability_max_blocking_time_nanosec.description: Number of nanoseconds for max blocking time.

sendBufferSize.description: Size in bytes of the send socket buffer. If the value is zero then Fast DDS will use the system default socket size.

receiveBufferSize.description: Size in bytes of the reception socket buffer. If the value is zero then Fast DDS will use the system default socket size.

maxMessageSize.description: The maximum size in bytes of the transport's message buffer.

maxInitialPeersRange.description: Number of channels opened with each initial remote peer.

non_blocking_send.description: Whether to set the non-blocking send mode on the socket (NOT available for SHM type).

output_port.description: Port used for output bound. If this field isn't defined, the output port will be random (UDP only).

keep_alive_frequency_ms.description: Frequency in milliseconds for sending RTCP keep-alive requests (TCP only).

keep_alive_timeout_ms.description: Time in milliseconds since the last keep-alive request was sent to consider a connection as broken (TCP only).

max_logical_port.description: The maximum number of logical ports to try during RTCP negotiations (TCP only).

logical_port_range.description: The maximum number of logical ports per request to try during RTCP negotiations (TCP only).

logical_port_increment.description: Increment between logical ports to try during RTCP negotiation (TCP only).

calculate_crc.description: Calculates the Cyclic Redundancy Code (CRC) for error control (TCP only).

check_crc.description: Check the CRC for error control (TCP only).

enable_tcp_nodelay.description: Socket option for disabling the Nagle algorithm. (TCP only).

tcp_negotiation_timeout.description: Time to wait for logical port negotiation (in ms) (TCP only).

segment_size.description: Size (in bytes) of the shared-memory segment. (Optional, SHM only).

port_queue_capacity.description: Capacity (in number of messages) available to every Listener (Optional, SHM only).

healthy_check_timeout_ms.description: Maximum time-out (in milliseconds) used when checking whether a Listener is alive (Optional, SHM only).

tls_verify_depth.description: Maximum allowed depth to verify intermediate certificates.

tls_default_verify_path.description: Specifies whether the system will look on the default paths for the verification files.

intraprocess_delivery.description: Speed up communications between entities within the same process by avoiding any of the overhead involved in the transport layer.

port.description: RTPS port number of the locator. Physical port in UDP, logical port in TCP.

externality.description: Number of hops from the participant's host to the LAN represented by the external locator. Valid values: from 1 to 255.

cost.description: Communication cost relative to other locators on the same externality level. Valid values: from 0 to 255.

mask.description: Number of significant bits on the LAN represented by the external locator. Valid values: from 1 to 31 (UDPv4) or 127 (UDPv6)

max_msg_size.description: Maximum message size that will be specified in the transport layer. Valid values: from 1 to (2^32)-1.

sockets_size.description: Size of the send and receive socket buffers. Valid values: from 0 to (2^32)-1.

non_blocking.description: Whether to use non-blocking send calls or not. Valid values: true or false.

tcp_negotiation_timeout.description: Timeout duration for logical port negotiation. Valid values: from 1 to (2^32)-1. Only valid for LARGE_DATA mode.

scheduling_policy.description: Configures the scheduling policy used for the thread.

affinity.description: On some systems (Windows, Linux), this is a bit mask for setting the threads affinity to each core individually. On MacOS, this sets the affinity tag for the thread, and the OS tries to share the L2 cache between threads with the same affinity.

stack_size.description: Configures the thread's stack size in bytes.

max_bytes_per_period.description: Maximum bytes per period for flow control.

period_ms.description: Period in milliseconds for flow control.

use_default.description: If set to FALSE, a call to Log::ClearConsumers is performed.
